key_insight: Raising cold-spray pressure from 30 to 60 bar increased copper particle velocity, improving bonding and ductility while trading off hardness.
engineering_relevance: The study identifies a higher-pressure process window for producing copper CSAM parts with a better balance of conductivity, strength, and formability.
method: LightSPEE3D cold spray copper at 30, 40, 50, and 60 bar
Characterized deposits using XRD, SEM, hardness, eddy current conductivity, and tensile tests
Related pressure-driven particle velocity changes to microstructure and mechanical performance
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that higher spray pressure produced crystallite refinement, higher dislocation density, improved conductivity, and a clear shift toward more ductile tensile fracture behavior.
watch_item: The optimum window appears pressure-sensitive, so scaling to other feedstocks, geometries, or stand-off conditions may require re-optimization.
paper_title: Optimising Cold Spray Additive Manufacturing: Pressure-driven enhancement of mechanical performance in copper deposits
